The '''Sareeta''' were a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. They had dark, lidless [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and [[Color/Legends|pink]] skin. The species, [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sareet]], reached atomic-age technology before encountering the [[galactic community]]. The Sareeta leadership then encouraged their people to explore [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] with the hope they would later return to raise the development level of Sareet. One individual, [[Sassan Sareeta]], however, chose instead to pursue a career in [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]]. After aggressively running a [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in the [[Lazerian system/Legends|Lazerian system]], Sassan was recruited into the criminal corporation [[Syndicate One]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Sareeta were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] in form, with two [[Limb/Legends|legs]] on which they stood upright, and two arms. These ended in five-fingered hands, including an opposable thumb. Sareeta heads had rounded craniums, but heavy jowls gave their faces an angular look. These jowls had three dark spots on them. In the center of their face was a small, dark, [[Nose/Legends|nose]]-like depression, above which were two dark, lidless [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Sareeta had mottled skin with [[Color/Legends|pink]] pigmentation.<ref name="Yard" />

==History==
The Sareeta evolved on [[Sareet]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Yard" /> located in the [[Stensen sector/Legends|Stensen sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Their society developed in isolation up to the atomic age,<ref name="Yard" /> at which point, sometime between [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000]] and [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]],<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> they encountered the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] community. The Sareeta leadership responded to this contact by encouraging their youth to venture beyond their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and explore the new environment before returning to help in the development of their planet. However, not all juvenile Sareeta were so responsible. One such youth was [[Sassan Sareeta|Sassan]], who came to be known as "Sassan Sareeta" or "Sassan the Sareeta." [[Gender/Legends|He]] had no interest in building up his society, instead seeking free travel on disreputable freight haulers. Sassan developed an appreciation for the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]]-designed [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighters]], eventually constructing his own from three separate [[Starship/Legends|starships]].<ref name="Yard" />

Sassan shipped cargo on his [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Moondrake]]'', to dangerous locales. While [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] cargo to the [[Lazerian system/Legends|Lazerian system]] on the [[Harrin Trade Corridor]], Sassan encountered a picket line of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE starfighters]]. Instead of fleeing, Sassan allocated all power to his forward [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and rammed through several [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Following that incident, Sassan gained a reputation as a [[blockade runner]], which brought the Sareeta to the attention of [[Saujutta-Ok-Topii]], the head of the criminal organization known as [[Syndicate One]]. Sassan eventually reached a position within the organization supervising the corporation's transport regime and calculating jump routes for the gun-running operations.<ref name="Yard" />

By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the area around the Sareeta homeworld averaged less than one million inhabitants per inhabited planet. By [[137 ABY]], their homeworld fell within territory loyal to [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Atlas" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Christopher M. Olson]] created the Sareeta for the article "[[The Yard of Opportunity]]," which was published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 8|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 8]] by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. [[Erik K. Olsen]] provided an illustration of Sassan the Sareeta, in which the mercenary has a loose flap around his neck. It is unclear from this illustration whether this is simply part of his uniform or is some form of [[Wikipedia:Dewlap|dewlap]]. The ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] in [[2003]], indicated that "Sassan Sareeta" was an example name of a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]], despite this latter species hailing from the [[Sullust system/Legends|Sullust system]].<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>
